review_title: Great for both novice and experienced CRM users
description: Prior to implementing a CRM, we had no way to capture all of the sales intelligence our sales team had on our customers. Some reps kept paper notes, some used one note, some kept it in their head. With having an older sales force and facing upcoming retirements, we needed an easy solution to start capturing this data. SalesOutlook has done just that. The implementation was seamless and the customer service is outstanding. Quick response by [SENSITIVE CONTENT] every time we have a question or an issue.
author: Dana S.
pros: SalesOutlook is our company's first CRM system, so we needed to find a system that not only met our needs from a sales and management standpoint, but also was easy to understand and use for our tenured sales reps who have never worked in a CRM. SalesOutlook fit the bill. We also love that it is integrated into Outlook, so the sales reps do not need to sign into a separate system.
cons: For our situation, SalesOutlook fits all of our needs.
author_duration: Used the software for: 1-2 years

review_title: Outlook-Friendly CRM That Keeps Things Simple
description: Sales Outlook CRM is easy to use, especially if you already work in Outlook. Setup is quick, and it’s simple to turn emails and meetings into contacts, companies, and deals. The pipeline view is clear, search is fast, and basic reports are enough for weekly check-ins. It’s not great for heavy customization or complex automations, and integrations outside the Microsoft world can be limited. Mobile works but isn’t amazing. Overall, it’s a solid, no-frills CRM for teams that live in their inbox and want something straightforward without a long setup or training. Overall easy on the learning curve.
author: Cindy H.
pros: As a job recruiter what I like best is not having to switch in Outlook: Being able to turn any email or meeting into a tracked candidate or client record without leaving the inbox is the biggest win. It feels like a natural extension of Outlook rather than “another tool.”
cons: Mobile use, automation and reporting need improvement. Mobile experience is just okay: You can do essentials on the go, but heavy recruiters who live on mobile may find it cramped or slower than best-in-class mobile apps. Automation isn’t very rich: Simple workflows and reminders are fine, but advanced, branching automations, multi-step cadence builders, and lead/candidate scoring are limited without add-ons. Reporting and analytics are basic: Good for pipeline snapshots, but not for advanced forecasting, cohort analysis, or custom dashboards without exporting to Excel/Power BI.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months
